admin.visa_notes: EU citizen entering from Schengen — no checks, no stamps, no 90-day count. Bulgaria full Schengen member since 2025-01-01.
admin.schengen_note: Full Schengen. No border crossing warnings on this route.
admin.insurance_reminder: EHIC does NOT cover mountain rescue. Get supplemental Bulgarian mountain insurance (PSS coverage) before departure — a few EUR for the week. Required for Pirin and Rila hikes.

tasks.title: Book Panichishte accommodation for Jun 27–28
tasks.how: Seven Rila Lakes weekend is peak; Sofia Live Festival same weekend causes spillover. Book in advance.

tasks.title: Book Bansko hostel (Avalon Co-Living or similar) for Jun 24–26
Confirm narrow-gauge timetable the evening before the Bansko leg
tasks.how: bileti.bdz.bg or station board. Catch 08:55 Rodopi from Septemvri; depart Sofia ~06:30.

tasks.title: Arrange Bansko → Panichishte relay transport (Sat Jun 27)
tasks.how: Relay: Bansko→Blagoevgrad minibus → Dupnitsa bus/train → Sapareva Banya minibus → Panichishte taxi. Allow 3–4h; start early (weekend frequency drops).

tasks.title: Arrange Panichishte → Sofia return (Mon Jun 29)
tasks.how: Morning taxi Panichishte→Dupnitsa (~€15, ~30 min) then Union Ivkoni bus (~1h15). Depart by 07:30 for ~09:45 Sofia arrival.

critical.title: Mountain rescue insurance — buy before departure
critical.how: Supplemental Bulgarian mountain insurance covering PSS rescue in Pirin and Rila. EHIC does not cover this. A few EUR for the week.

critical.title: Download Mapy.cz offline maps (Bulgaria – South-West)
critical.how: Open Mapy.cz → download 'Bulgaria – South-West' region. Also download Sofia on Google Maps. Do before departing Finland — signal lost on the narrow-gauge leg.

critical.title: Check Finnish SIM EU data cap
critical.how: 12h/week remote work with video calls burns the EU roaming fair-use allowance faster than standard travel. Confirm your carrier's cap.

critical.title: Withdraw euro cash in Sofia (and top up in Bansko)
critical.how: Many mountain huts, village mehanas, and market stalls are card-averse. No reliable ATMs in Panichishte or at the chalets. Withdraw from bank-affiliated ATM in Sofia; top up in Bansko before Rila leg.

trip.name: Sofia
trip.start: Zlatnite Mostove + Boyana Waterfall — city-edge Vitosha half-day; boulder field, waterfall, treeline, no tourists.

connectivity.esim_note: Finnish SIM roams full 4G/5G in Sofia centre. No extra charge.

trip.offline_map_area: Google Maps — download Sofia region. Mapy.cz not needed for Sofia streets.
trip.borders: No border crossing. Within Bulgaria / Schengen.
trip.intro: Sofia rewards the non-tourist approach: base in Oborishte, skip Vitosha Boulevard, and you find a leafy embassy-quarter neighborhood with great cafes, the best banitsa in Bulgaria, and a working locals' market two blocks away. Two days is the right amount — one serious Vitosha morning, one afternoon of central culture, two good evenings out.

tasks.title: Ask hostel about Bus 66 last return time (for Vitosha hike)
tasks.how: Do not attempt Cherni Vrah (#A5) without confirming last bus back. Default to Zlatnite Mostove which has confirmed logistics.

tasks.title: Confirm narrow-gauge timetable (Septemvri → Dobrinishte)
tasks.how: bileti.bdz.bg or station board. Target 08:55 Rodopi; depart Sofia Central ~06:30.

resources.title: Sofia Transport app
resources.why: Bus + metro schedules including Bus 66 to Vitosha. Download before arrival.

resources.title: bileti.bdz.bg
resources.why: BDZ rail timetable and ticket booking for the narrow-gauge leg.

foodToTry.name: Banitsa
foodToTry.what: Flaky phyllo pastry filled with sirene cheese (or spinach, egg). The Bulgarian breakfast.
foodToTry.how: Hot from the oven at 06:00 at Banicharnitsa. Not from a cafe.

foodToTry.name: Shkembe chorba
foodToTry.what: Tripe soup with garlic, vinegar, and chili. Morning hangover standard and a Sofia institution.

foodToTry.name: Tarator
foodToTry.what: Cold yogurt and cucumber soup. Summer staple, refreshing after morning heat.

foodToTry.name: Lyutenitsa
foodToTry.what: Roasted pepper and tomato relish. Shows up on every table.
foodToTry.how: Eat with bread, not as a course.

foodToTry.name: Meshana skara
foodToTry.what: Mixed grill plate. The staple dinner.
foodToTry.how: Order the solo version at a neighbourhood grill like SkaraBar 1.

do.hook: Boulder river walk + waterfall loop on Vitosha's flanks; sub-alpine, shaded, non-tourist, city-edge.
do.best_time: Depart before 09:00; finish before midday heat and storm risk
do.find_it: Bus 66 from Hladilnika terminal → Zlatnite Mostove stop; confirm schedule at hostel evening before
do.image_query: Zlatnite Mostove golden bridges Vitosha Sofia boulder field
tips: Confirm Bus 66 last return time the evening before — ask hostel. Without this you cannot attempt the Cherni Vrah variant safely.
Bring 1.5L water; there is water at the waterfall but not reliably before it.
The fork to Boyana Waterfall is signed; take the right branch off the boulder field main path.

hike.last_transport_back: Bus 66 — verify current schedule at hostel

do.hook: The daily locals' market — vegetables, spices, street food, the city's social heartbeat.
do.find_it: bul. Stefan Stambolov 1202; tram/metro to Serdika then walk
do.image_query: Zhenski Pazar Women Market Sofia street food
tips: Pocket safety in crowds — keep phone and wallet in front pockets.
Hot food stalls (grilled corn, burek) are near the central aisle; produce is on the perimeter.

do.hook: Outdoor bar in repurposed canal space; backpacker and local young crowd; primary evening anchor.
do.find_it: ul. Knyaz Boris I area — ask hostel for exact current location
do.image_query: KANAAL bar Sofia canal outdoor social

do.nm: Ancient Serdica Complex + Banya Bashi Mosque
do.hook: Roman ruins at the Largo + the city's only working Ottoman mosque — central afternoon walking cluster.
do.hours: Serdica pedestrian level: open access; Banya Bashi: daily; indoor Serdica hours: verify on day
do.best_time: afternoon; avoid midday heat in the open Largo
do.find_it: Serdika metro station is the focal point; mosque immediately adjacent
do.image_query: Ancient Serdica archaeological complex Sofia Largo
tips: Serdica indoor access hours are not posted online — confirm on the day. The pedestrian level is always open.
Banya Bashi is a working mosque; cover shoulders and remove shoes to enter.
Combine with Sveta Nedelya church and the Sephardic synagogue for the full central cluster — all within 10 min walk.

do.hook: Outdoor sculpture park of communist-era monumental art + film archive; unique to Sofia.
do.hours: closed Mondays; visit Tue Jun 23 only (sole viable window)
do.best_time: morning Tue Jun 23 (only viable window — closed Mon)
do.find_it: ul. Lachezar Stanchev 7, Izgrev; taxi or bus from centre
do.image_query: Museum of Socialist Art Sofia outdoor sculpture park

do.hook: Anti-tourist art and social space; alternative crowd; evening option when KANAAL doesn't fit.
do.find_it: ask hostel for current address — relocates
do.image_query: The Red Flat Sofia alternative art space
do.rsNote: Location and current programming unverified — confirm with hostel on arrival.

hoods.d: Leafy embassy quarter; best cafe and bakery concentration; low tourist density. Primary base zone.

hoods.nm: Women's Market area (Stefan Stambolov / Zhenski Pazar)
hoods.d: Working locals' market neighborhood; street-food orbit. Daytime activity zone.
Ottoman/Roman/communist architecture overlap. Afternoon walking zone, not a work base.

trip.stayPick: Hostel in Oborishte / Women's Market zone — best social backpacker atmosphere, walkable to the market, 10 min to Serdika central cluster. Work cafes dense enough for full Mon–Tue work blocks.

eat.hook: Highest-rated banitsa spot in Oborishte; Mon–Fri only — both Sofia days are weekdays.
facts: bul. Evlogi i Hristo Georgiev 102, Oborishte
eat.find_it: bul. Evlogi i Hristo Georgiev 102, Oborishte; walk from hostel
tips: If walking toward Women's Market / Serdika direction, use Furna instead — do not do both on the same morning.

eat.hook: Residential-area grill in Zaimov Park; mixed grill + mezze; away from all tourist clusters.
eat.cost: ~€7.50–11 solo meal (order the solo mixed grill, not the table set)
eat.best_time: 19:00 (outdoor seating fills by 20:00 on warm June evenings)
eat.find_it: ul. Dimitar Grekov 2; short walk from Oborishte via Zaimov Park
eat.image_query: SkaraBar 1 Sofia grill Zaimov Park skara
tips: Order the solo mixed grill (not the large table set) to stay within budget.
Arrive by 19:00 on warm evenings or be ready to wait for outdoor seating.

eat.hook: Assembled daytime lunch from market stalls — the Bourdain-style solo lunch option.
eat.image_query: Zhenski Pazar Women Market Sofia food stalls

eat.hook: Rotating soup counter; the work-break lunch — lighter and later than tripe soup.
eat.find_it: ul. Tsar Ivan Shishman 8, ~10 min walk from Oborishte
eat.image_query: Supa Star Sofia soup counter lunch

eat.hook: Tripe soup institution — stripped-down chorbadjiinitsa; cash only; Mon–Fri 09:00–16:00.
eat.find_it: ul. Dimitar Petkov 2, Sveta Troitsa area; 15 min from centre
tips: Cash only likely — carry small EUR notes.

eat.hook: Morning banitsa near Serdika metro — route-dependent alternative to Banicharnitsa.
eat.best_time: 07:30–09:00 (popular fillings sell out by late morning)
eat.find_it: bul. Stefan Stambolov 3, near Serdika metro exit
tips: Use if walking toward Women's Market / Serdika direction. Do not combine with Banicharnitsa on the same morning.

mobility.note: Walkable core; metro (~€0.80/tap) for longer transfers. Bus 66 for Vitosha.

timing.note: Jun 22–23 are weekdays — ideal for Sofia. Work blocks fit Mon–Tue. Sofia Live Festival is Jun 27–28 (after Sofia leg).

trip.name: Bansko / Pirin
trip.start: Sinanitsa / Muratovo Lakes hike — full-day Pirin alpine, clear mountain lakes, near-zero crowds.
trip.inLeg: Arrive via narrow-gauge → Dobrinishte taxi, Jun 24 ~16:00

connectivity.esim_note: 4G/5G in Bansko town. Patchy to dead zone on Pirin trails above Vihren Chalet.

trip.offline_map_area: Mapy.cz — download 'Bulgaria – South-West' offline region. Covers Vihren, Sinanitsa, Banderishki Lakes trails accurately.
trip.borders: No border crossing. Within Bulgaria / Schengen.
trip.intro: Bansko in June is the ski resort with the lifts stopped — cheaper, quieter, and the mountain access (trail shuttles to Vihren Chalet) works without winter crowds. Budget filter strictly applied: old-town mehanas and the residential Pirin Street zone, never the gondola-area tourist strip. Three nights = one serious hike day, one culture half-day, one second-hike or rest day.

tasks.title: Check Baba Vuna hours on arrival day
tasks.how: Ask hostel on check-in. Have Dedo Yonkata as fallback if closed.

tasks.title: Confirm Vihren Chalet shuttle schedule for hike day(s)
tasks.how: Last shuttle back from Vihren Chalet is the key constraint. Verify with hostel or at the bus station.

tasks.title: Pre-order kapama at Zehtindjieva Kashta
tasks.due: Day of visit (earlier in the day)
tasks.how: Clay-pot dishes often need to be pre-ordered. Call ahead or stop by at lunch to order for dinner.

resources.title: Pirin National Park official site
resources.why: Trail conditions and seasonal closures for Pirin NP.

resources.title: Mapy.cz
resources.why: Definitive offline trail maps for Pirin NP. Download Bulgaria – South-West region.

foodToTry.name: Kapama
foodToTry.what: Slow-cooked mixed meats (pork, chicken, sausage) with sauerkraut in a sealed clay pot. The Bansko regional speciality.
foodToTry.how: Order at Zehtindjieva Kashta — may need advance pre-order earlier in the day.

foodToTry.name: Vegetable sach
foodToTry.what: Mixed vegetables roasted in a curved iron pan. The local alternative to meat sach.

foodToTry.name: Garash cake
foodToTry.how: Find at a bakery in the old town.

foodToTry.name: Tarator
foodToTry.what: Cold cucumber-yogurt soup. Cooling after a hike.
foodToTry.how: Order at any of the mehanas.

do.hook: Full-day alpine circuit through Pirin's best off-radar lakes — far less crowded than Rila; clear teal water; no chairlift crowd.
do.best_time: Depart Vihren Chalet by 08:00; afternoon thunderstorms common after 14:00 — begin descent no later
do.find_it: Shuttle to Vihren Chalet (Bunderitsa) from Bansko bus station; trail starts at chalet
do.image_query: Sinanitsa lake Pirin National Park Bulgaria alpine
tips: Carry 2L+ water; no reliable water sources until the lakes themselves.
Total dead zone at Sinanitsa and Muratovo — download Mapy.cz offline before leaving Bansko.
Confirm last shuttle back from Vihren Chalet the evening before — this is the trip constraint.
Baikushev's Pine is a free 5-min stop en route; ask shuttle driver to stop.

hike.last_transport_back: last shuttle from Vihren Chalet — verify evening before

do.hook: The mandatory logistics hub for all Pirin high-country routes — altitude start point, condition check, water fill.
facts: shuttle from Bansko bus station ~€5 each way
do.find_it: on the Bunderitsa road above Bansko; shuttle will pass it
do.image_query: Vihren Chalet Bunderitsa Pirin Bulgaria mountain hut
tips: Carry emergency snacks regardless — chalet food availability is dynamic.
Check trail conditions here before committing to the route above.

do.hook: Lower Pirin lakes circuit — less effort than Sinanitsa; the recommended second-hike-day option.
do.find_it: same staging base as Sinanitsa — Vihren Chalet

hike.last_transport_back: verify shuttle schedule the day before

do.hook: Finest National Revival architecture in Bansko; painted frescoes and carved wood ceilings; arrive at opening to beat groups.
do.hours: Wed–Mon (closed Tue); all three Bansko days viable (Wed–Fri)
do.find_it: Bansko old town; ask hostel for current address
do.image_query: Velyanova Kushta Bansko National Revival house interior
tips: Arrive at 09:00 — tour groups start arriving from 10:00.
Combine with Holy Trinity Church next door (free, quick).

do.hook: The serious summit at 2,914 m — exposed rocky ridge; only if legs are fresh and weather clear.
facts: ~600 m gain from chalet · exposed ridge
do.best_time: pre-dawn start; summit before 10:00 before afternoon storms
do.find_it: Vihren Chalet staging base; route via Vihren Hut to summit
do.rsNote: Ridge conditions in late June unverified — snow patches possible on upper Vihren in June. Check conditions at Vihren Chalet before committing.
tips: Carry 3L water.
Check snow conditions at the chalet — upper ridge can hold snow patches in late June.
Only attempt if legs are fully fresh from the Sinanitsa day.

hike.last_transport_back: verify shuttle — same constraint as all Pirin hikes

do.hook: 19th-century church with Rilski Monastery school art; combine with Velyan's House in one short cultural cluster.
facts: adjacent to Velyan's House, Bansko old town
do.find_it: directly adjacent to Velyan's House in the old town
do.image_query: Holy Trinity Church Bansko old town cobblestone

do.hook: 1,300-year-old Bosnian pine — Bulgaria's oldest and largest tree; free roadside stop on the shuttle to Vihren.
do.best_time: en route to or from Vihren Chalet on any hike day
do.find_it: on the road to Vihren Chalet / Bunderitsa; marked; ask shuttle driver
do.image_query: Baikushev Pine oldest Bosnian pine Bulgaria Pirin

do.hook: Rain-day or rest-day backup — easy flat valley walk, no significant gain, works in light rain.
facts: NO direct bus — taxi or 45 min walk from Bansko
do.best_time: any time; works in light rain
do.find_it: NO direct bus to trailhead — taxi from Bansko (~€5–8) or 45 min walk. Arrange in advance.
do.image_query: Demyanitsa valley Pirin Bulgaria easy walk

hike.last_transport_back: return by same route or taxi

hoods.d: Cobblestone streets, traditional mehanas, National Revival houses. Evening and cultural zone; where the local food scene lives.
Residential zone adjacent to old town; some cafes; Zehtindjieva Kashta is here. Away from gondola tourist strip.

trip.stayPick: Avalon Co-Living or similar social backpacker hostel, old-town-adjacent. Check availability for Jun 24–26 before trip locks in. Avoid gondola-strip hotels entirely.

eat.hook: Primary Bansko mehana; explicitly off the gondola tourist strip; kapama, clay-pot dishes, live folk music some evenings.
eat.find_it: ul. Georgi Kovachev 11, Novia grad side streets — not on the gondola/tourist strip
eat.image_query: Zehtindjieva Kashta Bansko mehana traditional Bulgarian food
tips: Pre-order kapama or clay-pot dishes earlier in the day — they need advance prep.
Away from the gondola strip; ask hostel to confirm the current walking route.

eat.hook: Village eatery on the residential Pirin Street side; vegetable sach and cheap local dishes.
eat.find_it: Pirin Str. 42, residential side of the old town
eat.image_query: Dedo Tase Bansko village eatery local food
tips: Order the vegetable sach.

eat.hook: Arrival-night dinner near the rail/bus station — practical after the narrow-gauge journey; mehana format, no foreigner premium.
facts: ul. Patriarh Evtimiy 2, north end near station
eat.find_it: ul. Patriarh Evtimiy 2, north end of Bansko near the rail/bus station
eat.image_query: Banski Han Bansko mehana traditional restaurant

eat.hook: Cheapest venue in the dossier — canteen model, tray and point; hyper-local signal (Bulgarian Reddit); the primary daytime lunch.
eat.hours: UNVERIFIED — dynamic, may reduce in low summer. Ask hostel on arrival.
eat.best_time: lunchtime (arrive 12:00–13:00 before best dishes run out)
eat.find_it: ul. Tsar Simeon 16, old town, near Holy Trinity Church
eat.image_query: Baba Vuna Bansko canteen gostilnitsa local Bulgarian food
eat.rsNote: Hours unverified — dynamic and may reduce in low summer season. Ask hostel on arrival. Have Dedo Yonkata as backup.
tips: Cash likely; carry small EUR notes.
If closed, use Dedo Yonkata (#8) as backup.

eat.hook: Mountain hut trail fuel — pre-hike soup or tea on the way up; not a restaurant.
eat.hours: UNVERIFIED — opens early for hikers; dynamic
eat.find_it: on the Bunderitsa road; shuttle will pass it
eat.image_query: Bunderitsa chalet Pirin mountain hut Bulgaria
eat.rsNote: Hours and food availability unverified — dynamic based on what hut staff brought up. Carry emergency snacks regardless; do not rely as sole nutrition source.

eat.hook: Backup daytime lunch — emergency fallback if Baba Vuna is closed; lower signal quality but reliable hours.
eat.image_query: Mehana Dedo Yonkata Bansko traditional restaurant
tips: Emergency backup only — use if Baba Vuna is closed.

mobility.note: Walkable old town. Vihren Chalet: summer shuttle from Bansko bus station (~€5 each way, ~3×/day) or shared taxi.

timing.note: Jun 24–26 (Wed–Fri) are the best days for Bansko — weekdays, pre-weekend, no festival congestion. Ski resort off-season means cheaper and quieter.

trip.name: Rila / Panichishte
trip.start: Seven Rila Lakes — alpine cirque at 2,100 m; no comparable experience on the western Bulgaria loop.
trip.inLeg: Bansko → relay via Blagoevgrad/Dupnitsa/Sapareva Banya → Panichishte, Jun 27 (3–4h; start early)
trip.outLeg: Morning taxi Panichishte → Dupnitsa → Sofia bus, Jun 29 (depart 07:30)

connectivity.esim_note: 4G decent at Panichishte village and lower lakes / chairlift area. Signal drops sharply in upper lake bowls — total dead zone above the cirque.

trip.offline_map_area: Mapy.cz — same 'Bulgaria – South-West' download covers Seven Lakes circuit and Malyovitsa. Download before leaving Bansko.
trip.borders: No border crossing. Within Bulgaria / Schengen.
trip.intro: Panichishte is a small base village in the Rila foothills — the jumping-off point for the Seven Lakes and Rila Monastery. Two nights is exactly right: one full day for the high-country circuit (Saturday), one day for Rila Monastery (Sunday). Weekend crowds at the Lakes are real; the mitigation is early start. Signal in the upper bowls is dead — offline maps essential.

tasks.title: Confirm Pionerska chairlift hours for Saturday morning
tasks.due: Fri Jun 26 evening (or Sat morning earliest)
tasks.how: Ask Panichishte accommodation. First cabin timing determines whether early-start crowd mitigation is viable.

tasks.title: Arrange Rila Monastery transport for Sunday
tasks.how: Taxi (~€15–20 each way) or ask accommodation about a shared shuttle. 15–20 km from Panichishte; confirm route.

resources.title: Bulgarian Mountain Rescue Service (PSS)
resources.why: Emergency mountain rescue for Rila. Save number before departure: +359 2 963 2000.

resources.title: Mapy.cz
resources.why: Same Bulgaria – South-West offline region covers Seven Lakes circuit and Malyovitsa trails.

foodToTry.name: Rilska pastarva (trout)
foodToTry.what: Freshwater trout from mountain streams; the defining Rila dish.
foodToTry.how: Order grilled at Valyavitsa — the must-order item.

foodToTry.name: Kavarma
foodToTry.what: Slow-cooked meat and vegetable stew in a clay dish. The mountain restaurant default.

foodToTry.name: Popara
foodToTry.what: Stale bread soaked in warm milk with honey. Traditional mountain breakfast.
foodToTry.how: Ask at your guesthouse if they make it.

do.hook: High-country lake circuit at 2,100 m — glacial cirque, six connected alpine lakes; worth the weekend crowds with an early start.
facts: taxi to Pionerska base, 4 km from Panichishte
do.hours: chairlift: seasonal — confirm with accommodation evening before
do.best_time: Chairlift at 08:00 sharp (beat the day-trip crowd by 1.5h); begin descent by 14:00 (afternoon thunderstorms common)
do.find_it: Morning taxi/shuttle from Panichishte to Pionerska lift base (~€5, 4 km); chairlift from there
do.image_query: Seven Rila Lakes Bulgaria alpine cirque mountain circuit
tips: Download Mapy.cz offline before leaving Bansko — total dead zone in the upper lake bowl.
Carry 1.5L water; there are streams between lakes but treat or carry.
Fallback: if Sat crowds are genuinely unbearable, pivot to Malyovitsa (arrange transport via accommodation).
Tell someone your plan and expected return time before heading up.

hike.last_transport_back: chairlift closes at seasonal time — confirm the evening before

do.hook: UNESCO fortress monastery with exterior frescoes, carved arcades, and striped church. Sunday anchor — the default second Rila day.
facts: 15–20 km from Panichishte; taxi or shuttle
do.best_time: early morning Sunday (local visitors arrive but mass tour groups not until ~10:00)
do.find_it: Taxi (~€15–20) from Panichishte; or ask accommodation about shared shuttle. Confirm evening before.
do.image_query: Rila Monastery UNESCO Bulgaria frescoes courtyard
tips: Can pull to Sat afternoon if Seven Lakes circuit ends with energy to spare — Rila Monastery is below the high country, accessible same afternoon from Panichishte.
Cover shoulders inside the monastery complex.

do.hook: Mountain hut shelter and fuel stop within the Seven Lakes cirque. Essential logistics on the circuit.
facts: within Seven Lakes cirque; marked on Mapy.cz
do.hours: seasonal; open during main hiking season
do.find_it: within the Seven Lakes cirque; marked on Mapy.cz
do.image_query: Sedemte Ezera hut Seven Rila Lakes mountain hut Bulgaria
tips: Carry cash — card unlikely at the chalet.

do.hook: Documented fallback if Seven Lakes Saturday is genuinely unbearable — rugged alpine terrain, far fewer people. NOT a standalone second day.
facts: ~12–15 km · ~800 m gain · exposed upper section
do.cost: transport via accommodation (~€15–20 each way from Panichishte)
do.best_time: early morning start; same thunderstorm caveat as all Rila high-country
do.find_it: Different valley from Panichishte — arrange transport via accommodation the evening before
tips: This is a Seven Lakes FALLBACK — only if Saturday crowds are genuinely unbearable.
Arrange transport via accommodation the evening before; it's in a different valley from Panichishte.

hoods.d: Small base village for Rila high country. No tourist strip; everything oriented toward the mountain. Not a work zone.

trip.stayPick: Panichishte village hostel or small guesthouse. Book in advance — Seven Rila Lakes weekend is peak; Jun 27–28 availability may be thin. ATMs unreliable — withdraw euros in Sofia/Bansko.

eat.hook: Post-hike dinner with rilska pastarva — mountain trout grilled fresh; the Rila experience meal.
eat.cost: ~€7.50–11 (freshly grilled rilska pastarva is the must-order)
eat.best_time: 19:00–20:00 post-hike; Sat/Sun are busy nights — lower early-closure risk
eat.find_it: Mestnost Valyavitsa, Sapareva Banya area; taxi from Panichishte (~€8–10, ~15 km); confirm route with accommodation
eat.image_query: Valyavitsa restaurant Rila Sapareva Banya trout
tips: Order the rilska pastarva (trout) — this is the must-order.
At the hard cap (~€11) — worth it; this is an experience, not just a meal.

mobility.note: Morning taxi to Pionerska lift base (~€5, 4 km); chairlift (~€15 round trip) for Seven Lakes. Return leg (Jun 29): morning taxi to Dupnitsa then bus to Sofia.

timing.note: Jun 27–28 is the highest-traffic weekend at Seven Lakes. Mitigation: chairlift at 08:00, at lakes before 09:30. Operator accepted this tradeoff.

legs.spec: BDZ mainline Sofia→Septemvri (~2h, ~€4.50) + narrow-gauge Septemvri→Dobrinishte (~4.5h, ~€3.50) + taxi to Bansko (~€5–8)
legs.ds: Depart Sofia Central ~06:30; catch 08:55 Rodopi narrow-gauge from Septemvri. Three narrow-gauge departures: 08:55, 12:40, 16:25 — catch the 08:55. Show-up-and-pay reliable; no advance booking required.
legs.warn: No A/C on vintage carriages — catch the 08:55 to avoid afternoon heat. Confirm timetable the evening before on bileti.bdz.bg.

legs.spec: Bansko→Blagoevgrad minibus (~1h, ~€2) → Dupnitsa bus/train (~45 min, ~€2) → Sapareva Banya minibus (~30 min, ~€1.50) → Panichishte taxi (~20 min, ~€5). Total ~€13–15.
legs.ds: Allow 3–4h total. Show-up-and-pay for all legs. Start early — Dupnitsa→Sapareva Banya frequency drops on weekends.
legs.warn: Weekend frequency reduction on the Dupnitsa–Sapareva Banya leg. Start no later than 09:00 from Bansko.

legs.spec: Morning taxi Panichishte→Dupnitsa (~30 min, ~€15) + Union Ivkoni bus Dupnitsa→Sofia Central (~1h15, ~€5). Total ~€20.
legs.ds: Earliest realistic arrival: depart Panichishte 07:30 → Dupnitsa 08:00 → 08:30 bus → Sofia ~09:45. Factor into any departure flights.
legs.warn: This is a real Monday early departure. Set alarm.
